note: Provenance for the Bedrock/OpenAI custody/audit pack. This pack INSTANTIATES the vendor-neutral pack shape proven by packs/mcp-audit-trail/v0.1/: it reuses the same canonical SRS envelope schema, the same envelope invariants, the same Option A / decision_ref discipline, the same boundary_type routing value, and the same deterministic adapter shape; it does not invent a new receipt or conformance shape. The artifacts here are authored in this repository: a public-safe, SYNTHETIC, vendor-specific Bedrock/OpenAI model-invocation custody/audit input and the SRS envelope receipt deterministically built from it. They are not copied from any private corpus or external repository.

shape_reused: canonical SRS envelope schema (no re-vendor, no edit)
boundary_type as the top-level routing axis (audit_trail_boundary, pack-local descriptive)
GARP body under extensions.garp.body, named by body_kind
instantiates.differs_only_in: the explicit, vendor-shaped input (a Bedrock/OpenAI model-invocation custody/audit trail) and its body_kind name

not_a_live_integration.note: No AWS, OpenAI, or Bedrock API was called. No account, ARN, region, endpoint, organization, or credential identifier appears. Provider model references are synthetic placeholders (model-family-a / model-family-b), not real model IDs. Prompts and model outputs are recorded digest-only; raw prompts and raw model outputs are never carried. No model output is verified and no claim is made about the content, correctness, or truth of any model output.

public_safety.vendor_profile: bedrock-openai (explicit-file synthetic only; no live profile)

validates_against.note: The canonical SRS envelope schema and validator are the artifacts already vendored/reconciled in this repo; this pack does not re-vendor or edit them.

boundary_type.status: pack-local descriptive routing label, reused verbatim from packs/mcp-audit-trail/v0.1/
boundary_type.note: Not a newly-minted canonical boundary_type. The canonical boundary_type lane remains arcs-srs's to define. This custody/audit trail routes the same way the neutral audit trail does, so it carries the same descriptive value rather than introducing a new one.

artifacts.role: explicit public-safe synthetic Bedrock/OpenAI custody/audit input
forbidden-drift fixture (hoists a decision as a top-level verdict instead of using extensions.garp.body.decision_ref); same drift class as the neutral pack

standalone_verifier_arc.claim: A prospect can run the verifier offline from the receipt bytes, the explicit input bytes, this single stdlib-only script, and the schema assets already in this repo: no garp-local checkout, no install, no network, no credentials, no env vars, no API calls, no scanner, no service access.
checks: canonical schema bytes match the manifest's pinned sha256
schema/envelope invariants hold (same checks as tools/validate_srs_envelope.py)
artifact_hashes input sha256 matches the explicit input bytes
rendered plain-language reading matches the expected reading byte-for-byte
the top-level status/verdict drift fixture fails

non_divergence.guard: tools/test_verify_standalone.py runs both the standalone verifier and the canonical validator over the valid and invalid fixtures and fails if their envelope verdicts disagree; there is no second divergent verifier.
non_divergence.note: Envelope-validation logic is vendored into the single portable artifact so it ships self-contained; the parity test holds it aligned with the canonical validator.

standalone_verifier_arc.boundary_type_routing: The standalone verifier does NOT route on boundary_type; route authority remains receipt_type/body_kind/schema invariants. docs/BOUNDARY_TYPE_LEDGER.md remains the ledger for open-string values.

recorded_by: garp-sdk PR #78 (admission receipt contract conformed to Option A / decision_ref)
option_a.rule: Any decision/verdict semantics reference extensions.garp.body.decision_ref; never a body-level or top-level verdict/status/disposition/decision/governance_state.
